description: Draft full no-note corpus seed for issue 396 authority freeze assembly.
Machine-readable authority binding model for resolver and constraint-pack generation.

authoritative_semantics: The issue-specific authority pack MUST contain target scope, spec fingerprint, active bindings, active rule ids, full rule text, match basis, authoritative sources, shared contracts, downstream consumers, exception refs, required wiring, forbidden strategies, required validation, verification mode, verification method, oracle refs, and aggregate scope metadata. hash_pack.txt MUST be exactly one lowercase ASCII hex SHA-256 line over raw constraint_pack.json bytes.

Discovery resolver MUST fail closed on ambiguous symbol, duplicate semantics without allowed dedup, conflicting obligations, missing binding for authority-driven scope, missing verification contract, and pack recompute mismatch.
Final resolver MUST recompute the active authority pack from the authoritative corpus and MUST STOP on any mismatch, ambiguity, conflicting obligation set, or missing verification contract.

description: Constraint pack oracle. The implementation bridge artifact MUST carry a recomputable pack and a one-line lowercase SHA-256 hash over raw constraint_pack.json bytes.
Discovery resolver oracle. Ambiguous symbol, duplicate semantics, conflicting obligations, missing binding, and missing verification contract MUST stop the workflow.
Final resolver oracle. Pack recompute mismatch, ambiguity, conflicting obligations, and missing verification contract MUST stop the workflow.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 1. Scope and goals
statement: - verify isolation: each test MUST NOT leak state to another test

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 1. Scope and goals
statement: - verify isolation: each test MUST NOT leak state to another test

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 1. Scope and goals > 1.1 AMP-facing coverage contract
statement: - BadGuys MUST maintain at least one normative test path for each capability group.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 1. Scope and goals > 1.1 AMP-facing coverage contract
statement:
- A capability group MAY be covered by multiple tests; a single test MAY cover
  multiple groups.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 1. Scope and goals > 1.1 AMP-facing coverage contract
statement:
- BadGuys MUST NOT claim full runner coverage merely from a passing partial or
  historical matrix.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 1. Scope and goals > 1.1 AMP-facing coverage contract
statement:
- New runner-facing capability added to scripts/am_patch.py or its normative
  specification MUST be assigned to one of the capability groups in this
  document before related coverage is claimed.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 1. Scope and goals > 1.1.1 Expensive gate coverage discipline
statement:
- For expensive runner gate families whose execution cost can dominate suite
  runtime, BadGuys MUST prefer one explicit normative sentinel path over
  repeated incidental triggering in unrelated tests.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 1. Scope and goals > 1.1.1 Expensive gate coverage discipline
statement:
- When BadGuys claims coverage for the pytest gate family in G3, it MUST
  provide at least one dedicated test whose central evaluation proves that the
  pytest gate executed.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 1. Scope and goals > 1.1.1 Expensive gate coverage discipline
statement:
- The dedicated pytest path MUST constrain runner pytest scope to a targeted
  repo-relative test file rather than the full default tests target set.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 1. Scope and goals > 1.1.1 Expensive gate coverage discipline
statement:
- Tests whose primary assertion is not pytest coverage MUST NOT rely on
  incidental pytest execution for correctness claims. When such a test would
  otherwise trigger pytest only as a side effect, its runner invocation SHOULD
  pass --skip-pytest.

statement: Before executing tests, BadGuys MUST check:

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 10. Commit tests and commit_limit
statement: - if the number of selected commit tests > commit_limit, the suite MUST FAIL before executing any test steps

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 10. Commit tests and commit_limit
statement: The FAIL message SHOULD include:

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 11. Error reporting and determinism
statement: When a failure happens, the suite MUST:

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 11. Error reporting and determinism
statement: All paths in messages SHOULD be repo-relative where feasible.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 12. Compatibility and evolution
statement:
BadGuys MAY add new configuration keys and new CLI options in the future.
Any such additions MUST be specified in this document (including defaults and precedence) before being considered normative.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 3. Entry points and CLI > 3.1 Wrapper entry point
statement: Implementations MUST preserve this behavior to keep imports deterministic.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 3. Entry points and CLI > 3.3 CLI options (normative)
statement: BadGuys MUST support the following CLI options and semantics:

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 3. Entry points and CLI > 3.3 CLI options (normative)
statement:
- --config PATH
  - repo-relative path to a TOML config file
  - default: "badguys/config.toml"
  - the selected config file MUST be the single source of truth for suite-global
    behavior, runner-start recipes, and [evaluation], including [suite], [runner],
    [lock], [guard], [filters], [recipes.tests.<test_id>.steps.<step_index>] for
    runner-start keys only, and [evaluation]

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 3. Entry points and CLI > 3.3 CLI options (normative)
BadGuys Specification > 3. Entry points and CLI > 3.4 Resolution precedence (normative)
statement: 1) CLI explicit override MUST win

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 3. Entry points and CLI > 3.4 Resolution precedence (normative)
statement: - the effective include list MUST be: config.filters.include + CLI --include (in that order)

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 3. Entry points and CLI > 3.4 Resolution precedence (normative)
statement: - the effective exclude list MUST be: config.filters.exclude + CLI --exclude (in that order)

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 3. Entry points and CLI > 3.4 Resolution precedence (normative)
statement: - include MUST be applied first (restrict to include set)

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 3. Entry points and CLI > 3.4 Resolution precedence (normative)
statement: - exclude MUST be applied second (remove excluded names)

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 4. Configuration (TOML)
statement:
The config file MUST be valid TOML. Missing top-level tables MUST be treated as empty.
Unknown keys MAY exist but MUST NOT change behavior unless explicitly defined by this specification.
Behavior-changing configuration for BadGuys is split deterministically between this TOML file
and per-test .bdg files. Known TOML-owned behavior-changing tables are: [suite], [runner],
[lock], [guard], [filters], runner-start step recipes under [recipes.tests.*.steps.*],
and [evaluation].

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 4. Configuration (TOML) > 4.1 [suite]
statement:
- runner_verbosity (string)
  - one of: debug, verbose, normal, quiet
  - default if missing: "quiet"
  - when non-empty, MUST be appended to runner_cmd as: --verbosity=<mode>

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 4. Configuration (TOML) > 4.1 [suite]
statement:
- console_verbosity (string)
  - one of: debug, verbose, normal, quiet
  - default if missing: "normal"
  - MAY be overridden by -q/-n/-v/-d

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 4. Configuration (TOML) > 4.1 [suite]
statement:
- logs_dir (string)
  - repo-relative directory for per-test artifacts
  - default if missing: "patches/badguys_logs"
  - NOTE (normative): the engine MUST delete this directory at the start of each suite run, then recreate it

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 4. Configuration (TOML) > 4.1 [suite]
statement:
- copy_runner_log (bool)
  - default if missing: false
  - if true, and if the runner IPC result includes log_path, BadGuys MAY copy that artifact into
    the per-test artifacts directory
  - if false, BadGuys MUST NOT copy log_path (runner logs are human-readable)

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 4. Configuration (TOML) > 4.1 [suite]
statement:
- write_subprocess_stdio (bool)
  - default if missing: false
  - if true, BadGuys MAY write captured subprocess stdout/stderr into per-test artifacts
  - if false, BadGuys MUST NOT write subprocess stdout/stderr into per-test artifacts

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 4. Configuration (TOML) > 4.2 [runner]
statement:
- full_runner_tests (array of strings)
  - list of test_ids that are allowed to invoke the AM Patch Runner without --test-mode
  - default if missing: []
  - each entry MUST be an existing discovered test_id; otherwise the suite MUST FAIL before execution
  - duplicates MUST be rejected (suite MUST FAIL before execution)

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 4. Configuration (TOML) > 4.4 [guard]
statement:
- require_guard_test (bool)
  - default if missing: true
  - if true, the suite MUST enforce that guard_test_name is present after filtering

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 4. Configuration (TOML) > 4.4 [guard]
statement:
- abort_on_guard_fail (bool)
  - default if missing: true
  - if true, a failing guard MUST stop the run immediately (FAIL-fast)

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 4. Configuration (TOML) > 4.5 Subjects in .bdg
statement: - relpath MUST be repo-relative

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 4. Configuration (TOML) > 4.5 Subjects in .bdg
statement: - relpath entries MUST be read from the owning .bdg file, not from [subjects] in TOML

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 4. Configuration (TOML) > 4.5 Subjects in .bdg
statement: - if a referenced subject is missing, the suite MUST FAIL deterministically before execution

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 4. Configuration (TOML) > 4.6 Recipe boundary
statement:
- asset materialization data and non-runner step parameters MUST be read from the owning
  .bdg file; runner-start recipes MUST be read from the effective config selected by
  --config PATH

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 4. Configuration (TOML) > 4.6 Recipe boundary
statement:
- if a required subject declaration, asset materialization field, entry materialization
  field, or required runner-start step recipe is missing, the suite MUST FAIL
  deterministically before execution

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 4. Configuration (TOML) > 4.6 Recipe boundary
statement:
- if .bdg-owned authority is repeated in TOML, or TOML-owned runner-start authority is
  repeated in .bdg, the suite MUST FAIL deterministically

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 4. Configuration (TOML) > 4.6 [recipes]
statement: - asset and step recipes MUST be read from the effective config selected by --config PATH

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 4. Configuration (TOML) > 4.6 [recipes]
statement: - if a required asset recipe, entry recipe, or step recipe is missing, the suite MUST FAIL

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 4. Configuration (TOML) > 4.6 [recipes]
statement: - if `.bdg` repeats behavior controlled by [recipes], the suite MUST FAIL deterministically

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 5. Environment variables
statement: BadGuys MUST support this environment variable:

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 5. Environment variables
statement:
- AM_PATCH_BADGUYS_RUNNER_PYTHON
  - If set, and if runner_cmd[0] appears to be a Python executable selector
    (e.g., "python", "python3", "/usr/bin/python3", or a path ending in "/python" or "/python3"),
    then BadGuys MUST replace runner_cmd[0] with the value of AM_PATCH_BADGUYS_RUNNER_PYTHON.
  - Purpose: allow the runner to control the interpreter used for nested runner invocations
    when running inside environments where the default "python3" may not be valid.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 6. Discovery and selection
statement: 2) Files MUST be processed in lexicographic order by filename.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 6. Discovery and selection
statement:
3) Each test file MUST be a TOML document. The test id is the filename stem
   (basename without the .bdg extension).

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 6. Discovery and selection
statement:
4) The TOML document MAY contain a [meta] table. Supported keys:
   - makes_commit (bool, default false)
   - is_guard (bool, default false)

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 6. Discovery and selection
statement:
7) If include and exclude are both non-empty and overlap, the suite MUST FAIL with:
   "FAIL: include/exclude conflict: <comma-separated names>"

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 6. Discovery and selection
statement:
8) If guard.require_guard_test=true:
   - if guard.guard_test_name is present in the effective exclude set, the suite MUST FAIL with:
     "FAIL: guard test excluded but required: <name>"
   - if the guard test is not present after filtering but exists in the full discovered set, the
     suite MUST inject the guard test back into the selected list.
   - if the guard test does not exist in the full discovered set, the suite MUST FAIL with:
     "FAIL: guard test not found: <name>"
   - the guard test MUST be moved to index 0 (run first)

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.1 Structure
statement: - A .bdg file MUST be valid TOML and MUST contain at least one [[step]].

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.1 Structure > 7.1.1 Token substitution in strings (normative)
statement:
BadGuys MUST support token substitution in all string fields originating from a `.bdg` file,
including:

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.1 Structure > 7.1.1 Token substitution in strings (normative)
statement: Tokens are written as `${name}` and MUST be replaced before an operation executes.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.1 Structure > 7.1.1 Token substitution in strings (normative)
statement: - Within a single test execution, every `${now_stamp}` expansion MUST yield the same value.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.1 Structure > 7.1.1 Token substitution in strings (normative)
statement:
- Central evaluation rules MUST NOT depend on the exact `${now_stamp}` value.
  `${now_stamp}` exists only to make prepared artifacts unique.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.2 Execution model
statement: - The engine MUST execute the listed BdgStep objects in order.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.3 Runner result determination (normative)
statement:
When a step executes the AM Patch Runner (scripts/am_patch.py), BadGuys MUST determine the
runner outcome primarily from the IPC socket event stream.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.3 Runner result determination (normative)
statement:
- If an IPC event with type="result" is obtained, BadGuys MUST use:
  - ok (boolean) as the authoritative PASS/FAIL signal, and
  - return_code as the authoritative runner return code.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.3 Runner result determination (normative)
statement: - If no IPC type="result" event is obtained, BadGuys MUST fall back to the subprocess exit code.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.3 Runner result determination (normative)
statement: BadGuys MUST NOT use stdout/stderr parsing as the authoritative decision source for PASS/FAIL.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.3 Runner result determination (normative)
statement:
- For every step that invokes scripts/am_patch.py, BadGuys MUST capture the complete IPC event
  stream (NDJSON, one JSON object per line) and write it into the per-test artifacts directory as:
  logs_dir/<test_id>/runner.ipc.step<step_index>.jsonl

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.3 Runner result determination (normative)
statement:
- The stream file MUST contain the exact NDJSON lines received from the socket (no filtering and
  no reformatting).

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.3 Runner result determination (normative)
statement:
- For every runner-invoking step, BadGuys MUST compute a deterministic value_text string by
  concatenating the 'msg' field from every IPC event with type="log" in receive order, separated
  by a single '\n'. This value_text MUST be used as the step's evaluation 'value'.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.3 Runner result determination (normative)
statement:
- BadGuys MUST persist a structured terminal result object derived from the IPC `type="result"` event into:
  `logs_dir/<test_id>/runner.result.json`

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.3 Runner result determination (normative)
statement: - The file `runner.result.json` MUST contain exactly one JSON object and MUST NOT contain NDJSON stream content.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.3 Runner result determination (normative)
statement:
- If the IPC result includes `json_path`, BadGuys MUST copy that runner-owned NDJSON file into:
  `logs_dir/<test_id>/runner.log.jsonl`

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.3 Runner result determination (normative)
statement:
- If the IPC result includes `log_path`:
  - if `suite.copy_runner_log=true`, BadGuys MAY copy it into `logs_dir/<test_id>/runner.log.txt`
  - otherwise, BadGuys MUST NOT copy it.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.3 Runner result determination (normative)
statement: - The copy/write operation for `runner.result.json`, `runner.log.jsonl`, and `runner.log.txt` MUST occur eagerly when the valid IPC `type="result"` event is received, not after the runner subprocess exits.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.3 Runner result determination (normative)
statement: - After eager persistence succeeds, BadGuys MUST treat the copied artifacts in `logs_dir/<test_id>/` as authoritative and MUST NOT require the original source path to remain present after runner exit.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.3 Runner result determination (normative) > 7.3.1 Required AMP-facing observability primitives (normative)
statement:
To satisfy the capability groups in section 1.1 without moving expectations
into `.bdg` files, BadGuys MUST provide the following observability primitives,
whether implemented as step operations, executor hooks, or equivalent centrally
controlled mechanisms:

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.3 Runner result determination (normative) > 7.3.1 Required AMP-facing observability primitives (normative)
statement:
- text file read
  - MUST read a deterministic UTF-8 text artifact from either:
    - a repo-relative path, or
    - a path relative to the current test artifacts directory
  - intended use: runner result JSON, generated manifests, or copied artifacts

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.3 Runner result determination (normative) > 7.3.1 Required AMP-facing observability primitives (normative)
statement:
- zip entry listing
  - MUST return the sorted entry names from a zip artifact addressed by
    repo-relative path or test-artifact-relative path
  - intended use: archive hygiene and bundle layout assertions

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.3 Runner result determination (normative) > 7.3.1 Required AMP-facing observability primitives (normative)
statement:
- git status inspection
  - MUST support at least two scopes: repo_root and runner workspace
  - MUST return deterministic porcelain-style paths for the selected scope
  - intended use: workspace, promotion, and finalize semantics

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.3 Runner result determination (normative) > 7.3.1 Required AMP-facing observability primitives (normative)
statement:
- IPC command send
  - MUST send a newline-delimited JSON command to the runner IPC socket used by
    the current step
  - reply and control events observed after the command MUST remain available
    to central evaluation via the persisted IPC stream

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.3 Runner result determination (normative) > 7.3.1 Required AMP-facing observability primitives (normative)
statement:
These primitives are observability-only. They MUST NOT move command lines or PASS/FAIL
expectations out of TOML, and they MUST NOT move .bdg-owned subject/path/materialization
authority out of the owning .bdg file.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.4 Runner test mode (normative)
statement: When a step executes the AM Patch Runner, BadGuys MUST control runner mode centrally.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.4 Runner test mode (normative)
statement:
- For every step that invokes scripts/am_patch.py, BadGuys MUST inject the flag --test-mode
  into the runner argv (including informational invocations such as -h, --help-all, or --show-config).

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.4 Runner test mode (normative)
statement:
- If the current test_id is listed in config table [runner].full_runner_tests, BadGuys MUST NOT inject
  --test-mode for that test.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.4 Runner test mode (normative)
statement: - A test definition MUST NOT attempt to control runner mode directly via extra_args.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.4 Runner test mode (normative)
statement:
- If extra_args contains --test-mode for any runner invocation, the suite MUST FAIL before execution
  with a deterministic error message identifying the test_id.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.4 Runner test mode (normative)
statement: - For every step that invokes scripts/am_patch.py, BadGuys MUST inject the flag --test-mode

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.4 Runner test mode (normative)
statement: - If the current test_id is listed in config table [runner].full_runner_tests, BadGuys MUST NOT inject

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.5 Cleanup and isolation (normative)
statement:
2) Per-plan cleanup_paths:
   - each path in cleanup_paths MUST be deleted after the test plan finishes (pass or fail)

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.5 Cleanup and isolation (normative)
statement: Tests MUST NOT rely on artifacts from any previous test.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.5 Cleanup and isolation (normative) > 7.5.1 Subject-scoped workspace preparation (normative)
statement:
BadGuys MAY provide engine-level workspace preparation steps that operate on declared
subjects before runner execution.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.5 Cleanup and isolation (normative) > 7.5.1 Subject-scoped workspace preparation (normative)
statement:
- A step with `op = "DELETE_SUBJECT"` MUST resolve its target exclusively from:
  - `recipes.tests.<test_id>.steps.<step_index>.subject`, and
  - `subjects.tests.<test_id>.<subject_id>.relpath`

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.5 Cleanup and isolation (normative) > 7.5.1 Subject-scoped workspace preparation (normative)
statement: - The `.bdg` step for `DELETE_SUBJECT` MUST NOT embed filesystem path parameters.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.5 Cleanup and isolation (normative) > 7.5.1 Subject-scoped workspace preparation (normative)
statement: - The step recipe for `DELETE_SUBJECT` MUST allow exactly one key: `subject`.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.5 Cleanup and isolation (normative) > 7.5.1 Subject-scoped workspace preparation (normative)
statement: - If the referenced subject is missing, the suite MUST FAIL deterministically.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.5 Cleanup and isolation (normative) > 7.5.1 Subject-scoped workspace preparation (normative)
statement: - If the resolved path does not exist, the operation MUST succeed with `rc=0`.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.5 Cleanup and isolation (normative) > 7.5.1 Subject-scoped workspace preparation (normative)
statement:
- If the resolved path is a regular file or a symlink, the operation MUST delete it and
  succeed with `rc=0`.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.5 Cleanup and isolation (normative) > 7.5.1 Subject-scoped workspace preparation (normative)
statement: - If the resolved path is a directory, the operation MUST FAIL deterministically.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.5 Cleanup and isolation (normative) > 7.5.1 Subject-scoped workspace preparation (normative)
statement: - DELETE_SUBJECT MUST NOT target any path that is not declared in the owning .bdg [subjects] table.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.6 Forbidden patterns
statement:
- A test MUST NOT invoke "python3 badguys/badguys.py" (directly or indirectly).
  Reason: the suite holds a global lock during the run; nesting would deadlock or violate isolation.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 8. Logging, verbosity, and heartbeat > 8.1 Log outputs
statement: BadGuys MUST produce:

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 8. Logging, verbosity, and heartbeat > 8.1 Log outputs
BadGuys Specification > 8. Logging, verbosity, and heartbeat > 8.1 Log outputs
statement:
- per-test artifacts at: logs_dir/<test_id>/ (directory)
  - each test directory MUST contain a file: badguys.test.jsonl (NDJSON)
  - for each runner-invoking step, the directory MUST contain the corresponding
    runner.ipc.step<step_index>.jsonl file (see 7.3)

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 8. Logging, verbosity, and heartbeat > 8.1 Log outputs
statement:
The per-test artifacts root directory (logs_dir) MUST be cleared at the start of each suite run.
The central log path MUST be created (parents created as needed).

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 8. Logging, verbosity, and heartbeat > 8.3 Console behavior (normative)
statement:
- console_verbosity=quiet:
  - MUST suppress progress output
  - MUST disable heartbeat

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 8. Logging, verbosity, and heartbeat > 8.3 Console behavior (normative)
statement:
- console_verbosity=normal:
  - MUST print per-test result lines as tests complete
  - MUST NOT print step completion lines
  - MUST NOT print the debug configuration line
  - MUST NOT print live runner log lines
  - heartbeat MUST be enabled for long-running command execution (see 8.4)
- console_verbosity=verbose:
  - MUST print everything required for normal except the three MUST NOT clauses above
  - MUST additionally print one step completion line for each executed step using exactly this format:
    - "BadGuys step: test=<test_id> step=<step_index> op=<op> rc=<rc>"
  - MUST NOT print the debug configuration line
  - MUST NOT print live runner log lines
  - heartbeat MUST be enabled for long-running command execution (see 8.4)
- console_verbosity=debug:
  - MUST print everything required for verbose
  - MUST additionally print exactly one effective configuration line at run start using exactly this format:
    - "BadGuys debug: config=<config_path> console=<console_verbosity> log=<log_verbosity> issue=<issue_id> per_run_logs_post_run=<mode> suite_jail=<true|false> runner_cmd=<runner_cmd>"
  - for each RUN_RUNNER step, MUST mirror every runner IPC event with:
    - type="log"
    - msg present and string-typed
  - each mirrored runner log line MUST be written to stdout immediately upon receipt as exactly:
    - "<msg>\n"
  - mirrored runner log lines MUST preserve receive order
  - mirrored runner log lines MUST NOT add any prefix, suffix, severity, channel, JSON wrapper, or other metadata
  - non-log IPC events MUST NOT be mirrored to the console
  - raw subprocess stdout/stderr artifacts MUST NOT be mirrored by this rule
  - if a RUN_RUNNER step emitted at least one mirrored runner log line, all mirrored runner log lines for that step MUST appear before that step's:
    - "BadGuys step: test=<test_id> step=<step_index> op=<op> rc=<rc>"
  - heartbeat MUST be enabled for long-running command execution (see 8.4)

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 8. Logging, verbosity, and heartbeat > 8.3 Console behavior (normative) > Summary line (normative)
statement:
- console_verbosity=quiet:
  - MUST print exactly one final summary line:
    - "BadGuys summary: OK" or "BadGuys summary: FAIL"
  - MUST NOT include passed/failed counts

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 8. Logging, verbosity, and heartbeat > 8.3 Console behavior (normative) > Summary line (normative)
statement:
- console_verbosity in {normal, verbose, debug}:
  - MUST print a final summary line that includes counts:
    - "BadGuys summary: OK passed=<N> failed=<M>" or
    - "BadGuys summary: FAIL passed=<N> failed=<M>"
  - passed MUST equal the number of executed tests that PASSED
  - failed MUST equal the number of executed tests that FAILED
  - tests that did not execute (e.g. interrupted run) MUST NOT be counted as failed

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 8. Logging, verbosity, and heartbeat > 8.4 Heartbeat (normative)
statement: - Heartbeat MUST be emitted only when console_verbosity in {normal, verbose, debug}.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 8. Logging, verbosity, and heartbeat > 8.4 Heartbeat (normative)
statement: - Heartbeat MUST be written to stderr.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 8. Logging, verbosity, and heartbeat > 8.4 Heartbeat (normative)
statement: - Heartbeat cadence SHOULD be approximately every 5 seconds while a command step runs.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 8. Logging, verbosity, and heartbeat > 8.4 Heartbeat (normative)
statement: - Heartbeat content MUST indicate that the suite is still running a test step (exact wording is implementation-defined).

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 9. Locking
statement: BadGuys MUST use a process lock to prevent concurrent runs.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 9. Locking
statement: - acquire_lock MUST happen before initializing/clearing logs and before executing any tests.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 9. Locking
statement: - release_lock MUST happen in a finally-equivalent block so it runs even on failures/exceptions.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 9. Locking
statement: Lock file content (text) MUST contain:

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 9. Locking
statement: - on_conflict=fail: the second run MUST FAIL if lock exists

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 9. Locking
statement: - on_conflict=steal: the second run MAY steal the lock only if stale by ttl_seconds, otherwise MUST FAIL

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > BdG tests
statement:
Logical subject-to-path mapping, asset materialization, entry materialization, and
non-runner step parameters MUST live in the owning .bdg file. Runner-start recipes MUST
live in badguys/config.toml under [recipes.tests.<test_id>.steps.<step_index>].

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > BdG tests
statement:
.bdg MUST NOT contain runner-start command lines, runner-start overrides, or PASS/FAIL
expectations that are controlled by TOML. TOML MUST NOT contain .bdg-owned filesystem
paths, subject bindings, asset materialization, entry materialization, or non-runner
step overrides.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > Central evaluation
statement:
- For any step that invokes scripts/am_patch.py, evaluation rules MUST NOT use any stdout_* or
  stderr_* keys. The runner subprocess stdio is non-authoritative and may be empty.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > Central evaluation
statement:
- For any step that invokes scripts/am_patch.py, the evaluation 'value' MUST be the runner
  value_text defined in section 7.3.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 12. Error Handling and Status Codes
statement: - UI MUST surface mutation failures to the user using the JSON error envelope

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 13. AMP Settings Editor (Runner Configuration)
statement: PatchHub MAY provide a visual editor for the AM Patch runner configuration.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 13. AMP Settings Editor (Runner Configuration)
statement: - MUST reject writes when the runner lock is held (HTTP 409)

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 13. AMP Settings Editor (Runner Configuration)
statement: - MUST validate by rebuilding runner Policy from the updated TOML (roundtrip)

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 13. AMP Settings Editor (Runner Configuration)
statement: - From runtime version 1.12.12 onward, this editor MUST preserve list

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 13. AMP Settings Editor (Runner Configuration)
statement: - Reload MUST preserve element order, duplicates, and empty-string items

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 13. AMP Settings Editor (Runner Configuration)
statement: - Validate and Save without user edits MUST roundtrip each list[str]

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 13. AMP Settings Editor (Runner Configuration)
statement: - Chip deletion MUST be positional; deleting one duplicate MUST NOT remove

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 13. AMP Settings Editor (Runner Configuration)
statement: - The UI MUST provide an explicit action to append an empty-string item.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 13. AMP Settings Editor (Runner Configuration)
statement: - Empty-string items MAY render as placeholder chips, but their payload

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 13. AMP Settings Editor (Runner Configuration)
statement: value MUST remain "".

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 13. AMP Settings Editor (Runner Configuration)
statement: When a bootstrap-owned AMP policy key is typed as `bool` in the authoritative schema, the editor MUST expose it as a direct boolean control without requiring raw TOML editing knowledge.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 13Z. JSONL Governance and Repo Specification Editor (HARD)
statement: PatchHub MUST provide a dedicated full-page Editor UI as its own operator route for managed-repo authority corpora.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 13Z. JSONL Governance and Repo Specification Editor (HARD)
statement: - Header navigation MUST expose `Editor` as a first-class destination alongside `Debug`.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 13Z. JSONL Governance and Repo Specification Editor (HARD)
statement: - The editor MUST limit editable targets to `governance/specification.jsonl` and `governance/governance.jsonl` of the selected managed repo.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 13Z. JSONL Governance and Repo Specification Editor (HARD)
statement: - The visible primary editing surface MUST be human-readable. Raw JSONL text, raw JSON objects, and raw line-oriented JSON source MUST NOT be the primary visible editing surface.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 13Z. JSONL Governance and Repo Specification Editor (HARD)
statement: - The selected repo file is the only source of truth. PatchHub MUST NOT persist a shadow JSONL store, alternate cache-of-record, or derived editable state outside the current request flow.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 13Z. JSONL Governance and Repo Specification Editor (HARD)
statement: - PatchHub MUST generate a deterministic editable render that is distinct from the read-only audit render. `governance/render_master_spec_txt.py` MUST NOT be used as the authoritative edit/save codec.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 13Z. JSONL Governance and Repo Specification Editor (HARD)
statement: - The editable render/save pipeline MUST be lossless for every supported JSONL object type: `meta`, `binding_meta`, `obligation_binding`, `oracle`, `rule`, `capability`, `provider`, `route`, `surface`, `implementation`, `section`, `note`, `source_meta`, `workflow_step`, `workflow_transition`, `workflow_gate`, `workflow_invalidation`, and `workflow_rollback`.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 13Z. JSONL Governance and Repo Specification Editor (HARD)
statement: - The editor MUST support add, edit, and delete operations for every validator-supported object type named in this heading contract.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 13Z. JSONL Governance and Repo Specification Editor (HARD)
statement: - The editor MUST NOT expose unsupported object families such as `policy`, and MUST NOT create forbidden object types such as `source_line`.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 13Z. JSONL Governance and Repo Specification Editor (HARD)
statement: - Object add, edit, and delete operations MUST be dependency-aware and fail-closed. Save MUST be blocked whenever the edited corpus violates validator-enforced invariants, including but not limited to missing first `meta`, missing or duplicate `binding_meta`, duplicate ids, unsupported object types, forbidden fields, orphan rules, unresolved route/provider/capability/workflow links, invalid surface references, invalid implementation capability coverage, invalid binding-oracle references, workflow entrypoint conflicts, missing workflow transitions, missing workflow invalidation handling, missing rollback targets, or mismatched meta counts.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 13Z. JSONL Governance and Repo Specification Editor (HARD)
statement: - The editor page MUST include an operator-visible operations log. This log MUST classify messages at minimum as `Info`, `Warning`, and `Error`, and MUST surface load results, dirty-state warnings, validation start/result, save start/result, parse failures, validator failures, missing-file failures, missing-validator failures, and stale or external-modification warnings when detected.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 13Z. JSONL Governance and Repo Specification Editor (HARD)
statement: - The operations log MAY provide severity filtering and log utility controls such as clear and copy actions, but the log remains an observability surface only and MUST NOT become a second source of truth for document state.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 13Z. JSONL Governance and Repo Specification Editor (HARD)
statement: - Before standard save, PatchHub MUST execute this deterministic sequence: convert the current human-readable editor state into canonical JSONL, then run full corpus validation against the selected repo-local `governance/validate_master_spec_v2.py`, and only on validation PASS perform an atomic write to the selected file.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 13Z. JSONL Governance and Repo Specification Editor (HARD)
statement: - If conversion fails or validation fails during standard save, the editor MUST stop before write, MUST surface the failure visibly in the editor UI and operations log, and MUST open a dedicated helper modal.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 13Z. JSONL Governance and Repo Specification Editor (HARD)
statement: - The helper modal MUST show the concrete failure text, the affected object ids or references when available, and only remediation actions relevant to the detected failure class. The helper modal MUST NOT silently mutate the corpus on open.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 13Z. JSONL Governance and Repo Specification Editor (HARD)
statement: - The helper modal MUST support conversion-failure remediation actions including jump to the failing human-readable block, highlight of the failing field or malformed block, revert of the failing block to the last successfully converted editor state, deletion of an unsaved malformed draft block, and retry of conversion after operator edits.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 13Z. JSONL Governance and Repo Specification Editor (HARD)
statement: - The helper modal MUST support validator-failure remediation actions including at minimum: restore or replace missing first `meta`; create, merge, or remove duplicate `binding_meta`; rename duplicate ids; link an orphan `rule` to an existing capability, create a companion capability draft, or delete the orphan draft; create or relink missing rule, capability, provider, route, surface, implementation, workflow step, workflow transition, workflow gate, workflow invalidation, workflow rollback, or oracle dependencies as relevant; repair invalid `surface.route_ref`; repair invalid `surface.requires_capabilities`; repair missing `implementation.implements_route`; repair missing implementation capability coverage; repair workflow entrypoint, gate, transition, invalidation, coverage, and rollback defects; fill missing binding fields; and recompute `meta.counts` from the current document state.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 13Z. JSONL Governance and Repo Specification Editor (HARD)
statement: - The helper modal MAY provide an `Apply fix and re-run validation` flow, but only for fixes that are deterministic and fully described in the modal before execution. If a fix would create, relink, rename, or delete objects, the modal MUST identify those exact object ids before the operator confirms the action.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 13Z. JSONL Governance and Repo Specification Editor (HARD)
statement: - The editor MUST provide a separate unsafe save control for emergency operator use. This control MUST be visually distinct from the standard validated save control and MUST use an explicit unsafe label.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 13Z. JSONL Governance and Repo Specification Editor (HARD)
statement: - Clicking `Unsafe save` MUST NOT immediately perform the write. PatchHub MUST first open a confirmation dialog asking whether the operator is sure they want to perform an unsafe unvalidated JSONL save. The actual unsafe save action MUST execute only after the operator explicitly presses `OK` in that confirmation dialog.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 13Z. JSONL Governance and Repo Specification Editor (HARD)
statement: - If the operator presses `Cancel`, closes the confirmation dialog, or dismisses it in any other way, PatchHub MUST perform no conversion write and no file mutation.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 13Z. JSONL Governance and Repo Specification Editor (HARD)
statement: - After `OK` confirmation, the unsafe save flow MUST perform only this sequence: convert the current human-readable editor state into canonical JSONL, then atomically write the resulting JSONL to the selected target file without running corpus semantic validation.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 13Z. JSONL Governance and Repo Specification Editor (HARD)
statement: - The unsafe save flow MUST bypass semantic validation only. It MUST NOT bypass conversion, target selection, path safety, lock handling, or atomic write safety. If conversion fails, no file may be written.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 13Z. JSONL Governance and Repo Specification Editor (HARD)
statement: - The unsafe save confirmation dialog MUST warn explicitly that semantic validation is being bypassed and that the resulting authority file may become invalid for downstream tools.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 13Z. JSONL Governance and Repo Specification Editor (HARD)
statement: - After a successful unsafe save, the operations log MUST append a prominent `Warning` entry stating that an unsafe unvalidated JSONL write was performed.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 13Z. JSONL Governance and Repo Specification Editor (HARD)
statement: - Managed repo selection MUST derive only from `targeting.options` and the resolved target-root bindings already loaded by PatchHub. PatchHub MUST NOT maintain a second repo registry for this editor.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 13Z. JSONL Governance and Repo Specification Editor (HARD)
statement: - The JSONL editor MUST NOT commit, push, auto-create missing files, or modify any repo path other than the selected `governance/specification.jsonl` or `governance/governance.jsonl` target. This capability is a narrow repo-governance editor and MUST NOT widen PatchHub into a general-purpose repository file manager or change the existing `patches_root` jail semantics of the generic filesystem API.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 13Z. JSONL Governance and Repo Specification Editor (HARD)
statement: - The helper modal, remediation plans, operations log, and temporary editor state are transient operator surfaces only and MUST NOT become a second persisted authority representation.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 13Z. JSONL Governance and Repo Specification Editor (HARD)
statement: - The primary operator editing model MUST be a relation-aware workspace over the authoritative corpus. A whole-document editable buffer MAY exist only as a transient derived or service representation and MUST NOT be the primary operator editing surface.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 13Z. JSONL Governance and Repo Specification Editor (HARD)
statement: - The editor page MUST present distinct operator-visible workspace regions for corpus or graph navigation, selected-object detail, relations-and-impact inspection, and validation or repair status. The operations log MAY remain a separate observability surface, but MUST NOT substitute for relations-and-impact inspection or validation or repair status.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 13Z. JSONL Governance and Repo Specification Editor (HARD)
statement: - The primary operator surface MUST be task-first and guided. It MUST NOT require the operator to begin from raw schema terms, internal object families, or graph topology as the primary entry model.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 13Z. JSONL Governance and Repo Specification Editor (HARD)
statement: - The main workspace MUST use plain-language summaries as its primary operator language. Internal ids, raw refs, validator terminology, and schema terms MAY appear only as secondary, expandable, or detail views and MUST NOT dominate the primary surface.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 13Z. JSONL Governance and Repo Specification Editor (HARD)
statement: - Navigation MUST support selection and jump at minimum by object type, object id, inbound references, outbound references, validation failures, and workflow role or workflow step identity.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 13Z. JSONL Governance and Repo Specification Editor (HARD)
statement: - The selected-object detail surface MUST expose both local fields and the object's incident relation context. An isolated flat object form without relation context is non-compliant.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 13Z. JSONL Governance and Repo Specification Editor (HARD)
statement: - The editor MUST visually distinguish at minimum these relation classes: reference relations, coverage relations, and workflow relations. These classes MUST NOT be collapsed into a single undifferentiated links presentation.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 13Z. JSONL Governance and Repo Specification Editor (HARD)
statement: - For a selected object, the workspace MUST visually distinguish what the object points to, what points to the object, what the object requires, what the object covers, what the object invalidates, and where the object rolls back.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 13Z. JSONL Governance and Repo Specification Editor (HARD)
statement: - Workflow transitions, workflow invalidations, and workflow rollbacks MUST be visually distinguishable as different semantic relation kinds and MUST NOT be rendered as a single generic edge or a single generic fix class.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 13Z. JSONL Governance and Repo Specification Editor (HARD)
statement: - The primary workspace MUST be summary-first and progressive-disclosure. The operator MUST NOT be forced to read the full graph, all relation classes, or the complete technical ontology before understanding the selected problem, the recommended fix, and the expected impact.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 13Z. JSONL Governance and Repo Specification Editor (HARD)
statement: - Any mutation that changes identity, target references, coverage obligations, or workflow wiring MUST present an explicit pre-confirmation impact preview. This preview MUST enumerate affected objects, affected references, affected coverage conditions, affected workflow contracts, and whether the mutation repairs or invalidates any validator-enforced invariant.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 13Z. JSONL Governance and Repo Specification Editor (HARD)
statement: - The primary impact surface MUST explain in operator-readable language what will be repaired, what will be invalidated, what will remain missing, and what will change automatically. Counts of affected objects or refs alone are NOT sufficient as the primary operator presentation.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 13Z. JSONL Governance and Repo Specification Editor (HARD)
statement: - Rename, relink, and delete operations MUST be presented as relation-aware mutations and MUST NOT be disguised as ordinary local field edits. Delete confirmation MUST disclose inbound-reference impact, downstream impact, and workflow impact before confirmation.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 13Z. JSONL Governance and Repo Specification Editor (HARD)
statement: - The validation helper surface MUST identify the failing object, failing field or malformed relation block, the relevant upstream and downstream relation context, and the exact impact of each proposed fix action. The primary repair flow MUST be guided and MUST present the recommended safest fix before alternative fixes.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 13Z. JSONL Governance and Repo Specification Editor (HARD)
statement: - Opening a helper action MUST focus or highlight the corresponding object, field, or relation in the primary workspace. A text selection range within a whole-document editable buffer is NOT sufficient compliance with this highlight requirement.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 13Z. JSONL Governance and Repo Specification Editor (HARD)
statement: - Corpus-level invariants, including but not limited to first `meta`, single `binding_meta`, duplicate ids, unsupported object types, forbidden fields, and meta-count mismatches, MUST be surfaced in a dedicated corpus-integrity view and MUST NOT be represented only as local object-form errors.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 13Z. JSONL Governance and Repo Specification Editor (HARD)
statement: - The following are non-compliant as the primary operator editing model: a single whole-document editable textarea, a flat object editor without relations-and-impact inspection, a helper modal without impact disclosure, a local-object-only editor without inbound and outbound relation context, a graph-first primary workspace, or a schema-first primary workspace.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 13Z. JSONL Governance and Repo Specification Editor (HARD)
statement: - Transient graph views, relation maps, impact previews, repair plans, and workspace projections MUST NOT become a second persisted authority source. They MAY exist only as request-flow-derived operator aids over the selected authority file.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 2. Authority and Constraints > 2.6 Runs Indexing: Tail Scan and Cache (HARD) > 2.6.1 Background Indexer for jobs/runs/ui_snapshot (HARD)
statement: - PatchHub MUST run a background indexer task that maintains snapshots for jobs, runs, and ui_snapshot, and in `db_primary` it MUST maintain one persisted run-stats index split into `run_stats_meta` and `run_stats_seen`. `run_stats_meta` MUST contain at minimum `singleton`, `last_indexed_mtime_ns`, `last_indexed_filename`, `all_time_total`, `all_time_success`, `all_time_fail`, `all_time_unknown`, and `updated_unix_ms`. `run_stats_seen` MUST contain at minimum a stable source key, `log_rel_path`, `log_mtime_ns`, optional `log_size`, exact run timepoint, `result`, and the indexes required for dedupe plus cheap time-window queries.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 2. Authority and Constraints > 2.6 Runs Indexing: Tail Scan and Cache (HARD) > 2.6.1 Background Indexer for jobs/runs/ui_snapshot (HARD)

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 9. Runner Observation Model (Log + Event Store) > 9.1 PatchHub TOML config blocks for web-jobs DB (HARD) > 9.1.2 `[web_jobs_backup]` temporary SQLite artifact cleanup contract (HARD)
statement: When creating a verified backup via a temporary destination path, PatchHub MUST NOT leave orphaned temporary SQLite sidecar files in the backup directory after the backup lifecycle completes.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 9. Runner Observation Model (Log + Event Store) > 9.1 PatchHub TOML config blocks for web-jobs DB (HARD) > 9.1.2 `[web_jobs_backup]` temporary SQLite artifact cleanup contract (HARD)
statement: - Before starting a new verified backup, PatchHub MUST remove orphaned temporary SQLite sidecars from prior interrupted runs that match the configured backup destination template family plus the `.tmp` suffix.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 9. Runner Observation Model (Log + Event Store) > 9.1 PatchHub TOML config blocks for web-jobs DB (HARD) > 9.1.2 `[web_jobs_backup]` temporary SQLite artifact cleanup contract (HARD)
statement: - On successful backup publish, PatchHub MUST ensure that no temporary SQLite sidecars remain for the just-published temporary backup base path.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 9. Runner Observation Model (Log + Event Store) > 9.1 PatchHub TOML config blocks for web-jobs DB (HARD) > 9.1.2 `[web_jobs_backup]` temporary SQLite artifact cleanup contract (HARD)
statement: - On failed backup creation or failed verification, PatchHub MUST ensure that the temporary backup base path and its temporary SQLite sidecars are removed before the failure is surfaced.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 9. Runner Observation Model (Log + Event Store) > 9.1 PatchHub TOML config blocks for web-jobs DB (HARD) > 9.1.2 `[web_jobs_backup]` temporary SQLite artifact cleanup contract (HARD)
statement: - Cleanup MUST be limited to orphaned temporary SQLite sidecars and current-run temporary backup cleanup; it MUST NOT delete final verified backup files.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 9. Runner Observation Model (Log + Event Store) > 9.1 PatchHub TOML config blocks for web-jobs DB (HARD) > 9.1.2 `[web_jobs_backup]` temporary SQLite artifact cleanup contract (HARD)
statement: - Cleanup MUST NOT delete temporary SQLite sidecars when their corresponding temporary backup base path still exists.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 9. Runner Observation Model (Log + Event Store) > 9.1 PatchHub TOML config blocks for web-jobs DB (HARD) > 9.1.2 `[web_jobs_backup]` temporary SQLite artifact cleanup contract (HARD)
statement: - Repeated startup and interval backup runs MUST NOT accumulate orphaned temporary SQLite sidecar files in the backup directory.

heading_path: PatchHub Specification (scripts/patchhub) > 9. Runner Observation Model (Log + Event Store) > 9.1 PatchHub TOML config blocks for web-jobs DB (HARD) > 9.1.2 `[web_jobs_backup]` temporary SQLite artifact cleanup contract (HARD)

statement: Workflow-aware governance toolkit behavior MUST be specified in governance/specification.jsonl; governance/governance.jsonl remains the authoritative source of governance workflow data consumed by the toolkit.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > Workflow-aware toolkit behavior

statement: Workflow-aware toolkit components MUST derive next-step, gate, invalidation, rollback, and required_substeps guidance from authoritative governance workflow data and MUST NOT invent shadow workflow maps.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > Workflow-aware toolkit behavior

statement: If a workflow-aware toolkit component cannot derive required workflow guidance from authoritative governance data, it MUST HARD STOP rather than infer missing workflow structure.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > Workflow-aware toolkit behavior

statement: For pm_validator.py, the external-gate surface MUST be limited to pytest, ruff, mypy, typescript, and biome.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > PM validator external gates

statement: For a pm_validator.py external gate, the default execution model MUST be: untriggered gate -> SKIP; triggered gate with successful execution and exit code 0 -> PASS; triggered gate with successful execution and non-zero exit code -> FAIL; triggered gate that could not be executed -> UNVERIFIED_ENVIRONMENT.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > PM validator external gates

statement: For pm_validator.py external gates, a reported UNVERIFIED_ENVIRONMENT outcome MUST be treated as an execution outcome of that external gate and MUST NOT, by itself, be classified as a hard-rule blocked verification state for the overall PM validator result unless authority explicitly states otherwise.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > PM validator external gates

statement: For pm_validator.py external gates, the overall PM validator result MUST become FAIL when a triggered external gate is actually executed and returns FAIL; a reported external-gate UNVERIFIED_ENVIRONMENT outcome alone MUST NOT automatically force overall FAIL; existing hard-rule FAIL semantics for other validator rules remain unchanged.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > PM validator external gates

statement: pm_validator.py MUST accept an optional CLI flag --skip-external-gates.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > PM validator external gates

statement: When --skip-external-gates is active, pm_validator.py MUST NOT execute external gates and MUST emit SKIP for each skipped external gate with detail cli_disabled.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > PM validator external gates

statement: The --skip-external-gates flag MUST apply only to external gates and MUST NOT disable instructions/pack integrity and recompute checks, target checks, docs gate, git apply --check, Python compile, JS syntax, or monolith checks.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > PM validator external gates

statement: For pm_validator.py baseline invocation, INSTRUCTIONS_ZIP remains a required positional argument. pm_validator.py MUST aggregate verdicts across the full covered baseline validation flow and MUST NOT stop immediately after a non-prerequisite FAIL. If the instructions artifact is missing, unreadable, or the pack layer fails, pm_validator.py MUST emit explicit instructions/pack FAIL verdicts and MUST continue into every remaining deterministically runnable covered check.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > PM validator external gates

statement: When a later pm_validator.py check cannot run because an earlier prerequisite did not produce the required materialized input, pm_validator.py MUST emit an explicit verdict for that check with status SKIP and detail prerequisite_unsatisfied instead of terminating the run silently.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > PM validator external gates

statement: pm_validator.py MUST emit the final RESULT line only after every covered check in the current invocation has produced an explicit verdict or an explicit prerequisite_unsatisfied SKIP verdict. This aggregation rule MUST NOT weaken existing overall FAIL semantics for hard-rule FAIL outcomes.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > PM validator external gates

statement: For pm_validator.py patch-member collection, member-local checks that do not depend on a successful previous member verdict MUST be evaluated across the full member set and MUST emit all detectable FAIL verdicts before the validator decides whether apply-dependent stages are runnable.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > PM validator external gates

statement: For implementation_scope/final controller context, workflow-aware governance toolkit components MUST compute an effective prestart context consisting of the selected entry workflow step plus all recursively required_substeps derived from authoritative governance workflow data.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > Workflow-effective prestart context

statement: The effective prestart step order MUST be deterministic: entry step first, then recursive required_substeps in listed order, with first-seen-wins deduplication; from that order, toolkit components MUST derive effective capabilities in step order with first-seen-wins deduplication.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > Workflow-effective prestart context

statement: From the effective capabilities order, workflow-aware governance toolkit components MUST derive effective rule ids and effective full rule text in the same order; if a referenced rule object is missing, the toolkit MUST HARD STOP.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > Workflow-effective prestart context

statement: rc_resolver.py MUST emit workflow_effective_step_ids, workflow_effective_capabilities, workflow_effective_rule_ids, and workflow_effective_full_rule_text in constraint_pack.json without changing the meaning of existing active_bindings or full_rule_text fields; pm_validator_pack_contract.py MUST recompute and verify the same fields using the same traversal semantics.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > Workflow-effective prestart context

statement: gov_navigator.py MUST render authority-derived full rule text taken from authoritative rule.statement values, not only rule ids, in every navigator view whose content presents rule-bearing constraints either directly through rule references or indirectly through capabilities that trigger rules. At minimum this includes ENTRY SURFACES, ROUTE NAVIGATION, CAPABILITY NAVIGATION, IMPLEMENTATION NAVIGATION, ENTRY GATES, WORKFLOW ROOTS, WORKFLOW STEP DETAILS, and EFFECTIVE PRESTART RULES.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > Workflow-effective prestart context

statement: For implementation_scope/final controller entry, authoritative governance workflow data MUST expose a dedicated workflow root that carries prestart context via required_substeps; toolkit components MUST consume that root from governance/governance.jsonl and MUST NOT synthesize a shadow prestart map, and workflow_effective_context.py MUST be the single traversal authority reused by rc_resolver.py, gov_navigator.py, and pm_validator_pack_contract.py. In gov_navigator.py, rule ids MAY appear only as secondary references and MUST NOT be the sole normative content where authority-derived full rule text is required, and any JSON output that already exposes workflow root or step constraint data MUST carry the same authority-derived full rule text in the same deterministic order as the paired text view.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > Workflow-effective prestart context

statement: The canonical baseline invocations already specified in governance/governance.jsonl remain valid minimum pm_validator.py commands, and PatchHub load-time PM validation MUST continue to invoke pm_validator.py with --skip-external-gates.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > PM validator external gates

statement: This pm_validator.py external-gate contract MUST NOT reinterpret governance/governance.jsonl. Hard-rule blocked verification state semantics in governance/governance.jsonl remain unchanged for rules that authority already defines as hard-rule blocked verification state.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > PM validator external gates

statement: For pm_validator.py initial mode with an authoritative workspace snapshot, the validator MUST materialize the full workspace snapshot tree into the temp validation root before apply-dependent checks, compile checks, JS syntax checks, monolith checks, or external gates run. Changed-file selection remains the scope of validation, but the execution context MUST be the full snapshot tree, not a decision-path-only subset.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > PM validator external gates

statement: For pm_validator.py repair mode without --workspace-snapshot, the validator MUST enter explicit repair-overlay-only mode. In repair-overlay-only mode, the authoritative filesystem baseline MUST consist only of repo-relative files physically present in the authoritative --repair-overlay artifact, and patch decision paths MUST be partitioned deterministically into overlay-covered paths and overlay-uncovered paths.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > PM validator external gates

statement: In pm_validator.py repair-overlay-only mode, the validator MUST always execute all patch-archive checks that do not require workspace snapshot contents, including patch basename checks, COMMIT_MESSAGE.txt checks, ISSUE_NUMBER.txt checks, target.txt presence and format checks, repair overlay target extraction and equality checks, per-file patch layout checks, patch-member path and header checks, ASCII checks, line-length checks, and docs gate checks. File-content and temp-tree checks, including git apply --check, patch apply, Python compile checks, JavaScript syntax checks, monolith checks, and external gates, MUST run only for overlay-covered paths. For every overlay-uncovered path, the validator MUST emit an explicit degraded verdict naming that exact repo-relative path and MUST NOT abort the repair run solely because --workspace-snapshot is absent. In repair mode, the only snapshot-dependent checks are workspace-snapshot basename to overlay-target consistency and supplemental-authority file sourcing outside the overlay; if --workspace-snapshot is absent, those checks MUST emit explicit degraded verdicts instead of hard abort.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > PM validator external gates

statement: pm_validator.py, rc_resolver.py, and pm_validator_pack_contract.py MUST support a degraded validator mode for a repository snapshot or authoritative repair overlay that does not contain governance/specification.jsonl. In that mode, repo-spec-dependent resolver and pack-recompute branches MUST emit explicit degraded verdicts instead of hard abort or shadow-spec synthesis, while every remaining validator check that does not depend on governance/specification.jsonl MUST continue to run.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > PM validator external gates

statement: For pm_validator.py initial full-snapshot mode, repair-overlay-only mode, and repository-specification-absent degraded mode, the covered runnable validation flow MUST include every deterministically runnable check authorized by the available inputs for that invocation. pm_validator.py MUST aggregate verdicts across that full covered runnable flow and MUST NOT fail-fast after a non-prerequisite FAIL or degraded resolver/pack outcome; when a later check is not runnable because a required materialized input is absent, the validator MUST emit an explicit verdict for that check instead of terminating the run silently.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > PM validator external gates
